prompts_findings.prompt_lead: I'm building a knowledge memory for one specific challenge I'm working on. Do th

risks_fired.evidence: Three-beat numbered instruction with complex sub-questions; Claude narrates the approach before acting on beat 1.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Prime the student: 'Claude will narrate before acting; skim past the opening sentence and look for the connector list.'

risks_fired.evidence: Beat 2 asks multiple sub-questions; hardening added 'Ask one question at a time. Wait for my answer before asking the next. Do not show me the question list.' — structural guard in place.
risks_fired.fix_hint: No fix needed; guard is structural. Residual only if Claude interprets guard as ordering rather than interleaving.

risks_fired.evidence: Beat 3 asks for '2–3 working practitioners or specific articles worth reading (not vendor blogs)' but provides no evidence-level classification instruction.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Extend: 'For each practitioner source, state whether they have built the thing they are describing or are commenting on it.'

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: For every source in the curation plan we just agreed, create one file in sources

risks_fired.evidence: Multi-step instruction with four branching rules per source; Claude narrates the approach before acting.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Prime the student: 'Claude will recap the rules before starting; skim to the three lists.' (Residual med — intentional by design; audit IS the lesson.)

risks_fired.evidence: Prompt creates files at 'sources/<slug>.md' but specifies no overwrite behavior; Claude may pause if a file already exists from an earlier attempt.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add: 'If a file already exists at that path, overwrite it.' (Residual med — intentional by design; audit IS the lesson.)

risks_fired.evidence: 'NOT REACHABLE — share with me when you want this included' may be softened to a vague 'I wasn't able to reach this' without the explicit action cue.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Pin the exact string 'NOT REACHABLE' inside the sources/<slug>.md file format spec so Claude cannot paraphrase.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Read every real-content file in sources/. For each major topic you find, create

risks_fired.evidence: Prompt runs in plan mode; plan may inflate to 10+ bullet items before the student reviews, burying the topic split.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Prime the student: 'Look for the topic list in the plan; the file-writing steps are Claude's job, not yours to review.' (Residual med — intentional by design.)

risks_fired.evidence: A well-structured plan signals competence; students approve without checking whether the topic split matches how they think about the challenge.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Outer prose already has the forcing function ('Does the topic split match how you actually think about the challenge?'); residual risk is students skimming it. (Intentional by design.)

risks_fired.evidence: Rule 2 requires '[sources/<filename>]' per claim; Claude formats citations correctly but may cite files that don't contain the specific claim when a topic is thin.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Extend rule 2: 'Quote the sentence from the cited file that supports the claim on the same line after the citation marker.' (Residual med — intentional by design.)

risks_fired.evidence: Rule 3 targets generic observations but does not extend cite-or-flag to conventional-wisdom claims Claude treats as common knowledge.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add: 'Cite-or-flag applies to every factual claim, including industry-truism claims; if you can't cite, move to open questions.'

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Pick 3 memory pages at random. For each, is the top claim something specific to

risks_fired.evidence: Hardening added 'Assume at least one of the three has a generic top claim. Name it and propose one specific rewrite.' — structural forcing function removes the charitable-clear option. Residual: Claude may pick the mildest 'generic' to satisfy the letter.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Low risk post-hardening; student can push back with 'make the rewrite more specific to [my company/situation]' if the rewrite is still vague.

risks_fired.evidence: 'If on re-reading all three are specific, say why' creates accountability; Claude cannot silently pass. Residual: the explanation may be brief.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Low risk; structure is sufficient for the exercise's goal.

risks_fired.evidence: Claude may frame the generic claim gently rather than naming it bluntly; soft-pages.md list is a harder signal than a verbal hedge.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Low risk; the list write is a concrete forcing function.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Help me create my first custom agent as a markdown file in agents/. Ask me these

risks_fired.evidence: Hardening added 'Wait for my answer before asking the next. Do not show me the question list.' after 'Ask me these one at a time:' — full structural guard now present.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Guard is present; residual only if Claude interprets 'one at a time' as ordering rather than interleaving.

risks_fired.evidence: Starter rules offered with 'Change at least one so it's actually mine' — forced-customization mechanic present but mild; student may change a single word and satisfy the requirement.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Sharpen: 'Change at least one rule so it reflects a real constraint from your situation — not a rephrasing of the same rule.'

risks_fired.evidence: 'Show me the file before saving' creates an intentional confirm loop — by design, not a defect.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Design-intentional; no fix needed.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Read the agent file you just created, apply its role and rules, and use my memor

risks_fired.evidence: Prompt asks Claude to read two resources then ask for a task; Claude narrates confirmation of both reads before proceeding.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Prime the student: 'Claude will confirm it read the files before asking; look for the question, not the preamble.' (Residual med — intentional by design; audit IS the lesson.)

risks_fired.evidence: 'Cite which memory file each claim came from' without a sentence-level integrity check; citations format correctly but substance may not hold.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add push-back instruction in outer prose: 'If a claim cites a memory file, ask Claude to quote the sentence in that file that supports it.' (Residual med — intentional by design.)

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Take the source below and integrate it into the memory. Steps:  1. Read the sour

risks_fired.evidence: Prompt explicitly says 'Integrate its claims into existing pages (sharpen, don't append)' and 'Rewrite tops in place. Replace old framing; don't preserve it above a new section.' — strong structural enforcement reduces append drift significantly.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Outer prose already has the push-back mechanic; residual risk is low.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Tell me three pages that got sharper (not longer)' — Claude self-selects the reported pages and will favor any page where it made a change, not necessarily where the top claim actually improved.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add: 'For each page you name, quote the old top claim and the new one.' (Residual med — intentional by design; audit IS the lesson.)

risks_fired.evidence: 'One claim that got dropped or replaced' — Claude may report a claim was 'updated' when it should have been cut.
risks_fired.fix_hint: 'Drop any claim the source contradicts' is explicit; residual risk is low.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Review the memory. Find: two contradictions between topic pages; two claims that

risks_fired.evidence: Hardening added severity tiering (HIGH/MED/LOW) per finding plus 'name the one you are LEAST confident about and say why you included it rather than a more serious gap.' — strong structural forcing function. Residual risk is minimal.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Low risk post-hardening; student can push back on severity ratings during the approve/reject pass.

risks_fired.evidence: Six-item structure with severity labels and a forced least-confident declaration creates specific accountability. Residual: Claude may assign MED to HIGH-severity findings.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Student can push back: 'You rated X as MED — would this mislead a decision?' — approve/reject gate invites this.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Don't apply them yet — ask me to approve or reject each one' creates an explicit per-item student gate; rubber-stamping is structurally harder than for a polished plan.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Low risk; per-item gate is present.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Using my memory and the rules in the agent file, answer this question, citing wh

risks_fired.evidence: 'Citing which memory file each claim came from' without a sentence-level integrity check; citations format correctly but may not contain the specific claim.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add after the colon: 'For each citation, quote the sentence from that file that supports the claim.' (Residual med — intentional by design; audit IS the lesson.)

risks_fired.evidence: Prompt does not extend cite-or-flag to conventional-wisdom claims; Claude may leave industry-truism claims uncited.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Extend: 'Cite or flag every factual claim, including general observations — if you can't point to a memory file, mark it [FROM TRAINING DATA].' (Residual med — intentional by design.)

risks_fired.evidence: Short prompt; Claude may narrate 'Reading your agent file and memory now...' before answering. Low risk given the prompt's brevity.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Brevity mitigates; no fix needed.
